entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 27:14
entries.excerpt: A Typhonic wind sent itself against them. Another Latin translation says: A tempestuous wind. For Typhus in Greek is called inflation in Latin.

entries.title: Homily on Acts 53 on acts 27:14
entries.excerpt: Mark then the providential ordering of the events: first indeed, "when the south wind blew softly, supposing they had obtained their purpose," they loosed the vessel, and came forth; then when the wind bore down upon them, they gave way to it driving them, and were with difficulty saved.
